title: campylospermum schoenleinianum
intro: Campylospermum schoenleinianum, also known as the ivory nut tree, is a species of plant in the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Campylospermum schoenleinianum include its ability to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Campylospermum schoenleinianum can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the plant's compounds using a solvent to produce a tincture or oil.
